extracted_codes.context: ICD-9 code for CRSwNP (471.x), CRS (473.x, which does not specify CRSwNP or CRSsNP), or CPT code for sinus surgery for CRS

extracted_codes.display: Chronic rhinosinusitis (does not specify with or without nasal polyps)
extracted_codes.context: ICD-9 code for CRSwNP (471.x), CRS (473.x, which does not specify CRSwNP or CRSsNP), or CPT code for sinus surgery for CRS

clinical_criteria: Symptoms (e.g., nasal congestion or discharge) for ≥12 weeks' duration
Objective confirmation by either nasal endoscopy or computed tomography (CT) of the sinuses
One or more evaluations by a CRS specialist
Two or more office visits in the EDW
algorithm_summary: The CRS phenotype algorithm uses a multi-step approach: 1) Initial case identification using ICD-9 codes 471.x (CRSwNP) and 473.x (CRS) plus CPT codes for sinus surgery, 2) Control identification by excluding patients with any CRS-related codes or sinus CT orders, 3) Algorithm refinement requiring CRS specialist evaluation to improve positive predictive value from 54% to 91%, 4) Cases can be subdivided into CRS with nasal polyps (CRSwNP) and without nasal polyps (CRSsNP) based on coded data. The algorithm achieved 100% control PPV and 91% case PPV after iterations.
